content.problemStatement:
Create a program that simulates a to-do list manager using a queue. 

The program should allow users to perform the following operations:


Add a task to the to-do list.
If the task is already present in the list - then ignore the task
Display the current to-do list once all tasks have been added
The code given here has implemented queues using arrays. 

Update the code to solve the problem.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of tasks to be added. The next N lines each contain a positive integer task (1 ≤ task ≤ 1000).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the distinct tasks in the order of their first appearance, each on its own line. Separate the outputs of consecutive test cases with a blank line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All 10 tasks are processed. The first occurrence of each distinct task is kept, duplicates are ignored, resulting in the order 1 2 4 5 3 9.
First test case contains only the task 5, so only one line is printed. A blank line separates the two test cases. The second test case has all distinct tasks, printed in input order.

solution.approach: We need to keep the order of first occurrence while ignoring duplicates. Because the task values are bounded by 1000 we can maintain a boolean array seen[1001] that marks whether a task has already been added. While reading the N tasks we check seen[task]; if false we mark it true and append the task to an order list. After processing a test case we output the order list. This runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(1001+N) auxiliary space, which easily satisfies the limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) overall
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1001 + N) – a fixed‑size boolean array plus the list of unique tasks
